S70 (Model 7017) supports 4 to 12 processors; PCI bus replaces earlier Microchannel bus

metric_value: First 64-bit RS/6000 processor; commercial transaction processing optimized
notes: RS64 (Apache) is IBM's first 64-bit PowerPC-AS processor; designed for commercial workloads rather than HPC

metric_value: Aberdeen correctly forecast Spring 1997 PowerPC 604e SMP roll-out (RS/6000 F50 in May 1997)
notes: 'Last year Aberdeen forecast the Spring 1997 roll-out of SMP servers based on the PowerPC 604e chip -- which took place on schedule'

metric_value: Phase 1: PowerPC 604e SMP (May 1997); Phase 2: 64-bit RS64 high-end SMP (October 1997)
notes: Second phase of 1997 product announcements: high-end SMP server using 64-bit microprocessors and enhanced AIX

metric_value: Study positioned to review IBM Unix market position and S70 strategy fit
notes: Study purpose: review IBM Unix market position and strategy and where 64-bit RS/6000 S70 fits

metric_value: AIX remains active as of 2026; AIX 7.3 supported through 2033; one of last surviving commercial Unix variants
notes: AIX drives ~50% of IBM Power Systems revenue per IT Jungle 2023; IBM AIX 7.3 supported through 2033. Source: https://www.itjungle.com/2023/02/13/aix-the-last-standing-commercial-unix/

metric_value: RS/6000 S70 (Model 7017) was discontinued December 13, 1999 as specified. RS64-II (262 MHz) and RS64-III (450 MHz) followed as next-generation processors.
notes: post-study, RS/6000 S70 Model 7017 discontinued December 13, 1999. RS64-II (262 MHz) and RS64-III (450 MHz) followed. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IBM_RS/6000 | Source notes confirm: 'RS/6000 S70 Model 7017 discontinued December 13, 1999.' Wikipedia RS/6000 history.

metric_value: RS64 line discontinued in 2001 when POWER4 merged PowerPC and PowerPC-AS instruction sets
notes: IBM released POWER4 in 2001 and discontinued RS64 line. POWER4 merged the two instruction set architectures. Source: https://www.cpushack.com/2011/01/24/ibms-server-processors-the-rs64-and-the-power/

metric_value: Positioned as most comprehensive 64-bit UNIX computing solution available for commercial workloads
notes: IBM describes S70 as 'most comprehensive 64-bit UNIX computing solution available'

metric_value: Sun named as primary Unix competitor to RS/6000 in historical context
notes: IBM RS/6000 competed primarily against Sun SPARC/Solaris and HP PA-RISC in commercial Unix market

metric_value: Sun acquired by Oracle for $7.4B in 2010; Solaris/SPARC line eventually wound down
notes: Sun Microsystems acquired by Oracle Corporation in 2010. Solaris effectively ended. IBM Unix (AIX) outlasted Sun Unix. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IBM_RS/6000

metric_value: TPC benchmarks used to demonstrate S70 performance; estimated 18000 TPM competitive benchmark
notes: TPC benchmarks were the primary competitive performance metric for commercial Unix servers in 1997

metric_value: RS/6000 F50 with PowerPC 604e shipped May 1997 as Aberdeen forecast
notes: Wikipedia confirms RS/6000 7025 F50 introduced April 25, 1997 — consistent with Aberdeen's Spring 1997 forecast. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IBM_RS/6000
